inputs.userGoal: Launch a data ingestion pipeline using Azure Data Factory for importing sales data daily, transform using Azure Databricks, and store results in Azure Synapse Analytics.

reasoning.whyChosen: Required for Synapse and ADF staging/logging. HNS enabled for Data Lake capabilities.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Standard SKU is cost-effective for default usage.

reasoning.whyChosen: Requested by user for data ingestion orchestration.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Separate service allows independent scaling/lifecycle management vs Synapse integration.

reasoning.whyChosen: Requested by user for transformation. Standard SKU is sufficient for basic jobs.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Standard lacks some advanced security features (e.g., VNet injection restrictions) but is cheaper.

resources.name: syn-sales-analytics

reasoning.whyChosen: Central analytics workspace for SQL and Spark. Required for SQL Pool.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Requires a default Data Lake Storage account.

reasoning.whyChosen: Dedicated SQL Pool for storing analytical results as requested. DW100c is the entry level.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Dedicated cost vs Serverless pay-per-query. Dedicated offers predictable performance.

overallReasoning.summary: Standard modern data warehouse architecture: Ingest (ADF) -> Lake (Storage) -> Transform (Databricks) -> Serve (Synapse SQL).
overallReasoning.tradeoffs: Separate ADF and Synapse instances adds complexity vs all-in-Synapse. Databricks adds another compute engine to manage.
